content:
Overview

Municipal environments need hardened defaults, deterministic configuration, and audit-evidence that maps cleanly to SOC workflows. This write-up documents an operational hardening approach designed to eliminate configuration drift while keeping incident response practical.

Engineering Standards

Idempotent enforcement

Every hardening action is check-then-apply so repeated runs converge to the same state without side effects.

CIS-aligned posture

Network, authentication, filesystem, and service exposure are reduced with clear acceptance criteria and verification hooks.

SOC-ready audit evidence

Outputs are structured for ingestion and triage so operators can correlate posture changes with alert timelines.

Verification

Success means: (1) no drift on rerun, (2) required sysctl/fstab flags are present, (3) centralized logs contain consistent automation traces, and (4) the system exhibits stable, least-privilege behavior under load.
